title: Spec Task - DB-first runtime ownership with export-only file surfaces
description: Define that direct 1.0 runtime state is DB-owned in SurrealDB: workflow_runtime for epics/tasks/TODO/trace state, subagent_runtime for agent operational state, instruction/framework memory as DB slices, and project-memory as sync/export rather than framework boot-ingest substrate.

title: Spec Task - Unified export protocol for JSON data, docs, memory, and logs
description: Define canonical export surfaces from the DB-owned runtime for JSON data, documentation bundles, memory ledgers, diagnostics, logs, and parity/proof artifacts, including operator-facing rendering modes with plain/color/color_emoji output and clean fallback behavior.

title: Spec Task - Add VIDA storage spec for SurrealDB embedded backend and kv-surrealkv
description: Research epic: vida-2qe. Create the missing VIDA spec delta that explicitly decides whether embedded SurrealDB is adopted, and if adopted defines kv-surrealkv as the backend, decision boundaries, non-goals, migration impact, and proof expectations before implementation.
close_reason: Active specs now make embedded SurrealDB on kv-surrealkv the canonical storage direction

title: Spec Task - Spec-sync protocol for autonomous framework changes
description: Define the rule that when autonomous development changes framework behavior, the agent must check for nearby governing specs/protocols and add or update them in the matching context before considering the work complete.

title: Wave 6.3 - Final promotion proofs for VIDA 1.0
description: Produce the final promotion proof bundle required to declare VIDA 1.0 complete.

title: Spec Task - Instruction diff format and projection merge rules
description: Define the canonical structured diff format for instruction sidecars, along with projection merge order, conflict handling, and rebind behavior across migrations.

title: Implementation Task - Instruction slice runtime schema and sidecar patch application
description: Implement the minimal instruction-memory runtime on embedded SurrealDB with version metadata, immutable bundled artifacts, sidecar patch records, anchor validation, and effective projection support.

title: Wave 6.2 - Rollback-safe switchover controls
description: Add rollback-safe controls and decision surfaces for the primary switchover.

title: Protocol Task - Repeated-error web lookup rule for autonomous debugging
description: Define that after a repeated technical error or uncertain external API/format, autonomous debugging must escalate to primary-source lookup instead of repeating local trial-and-error.

title: Fix false cycle detection in br dependency wiring for linear backlog chains
description: br --no-db dep add returns CYCLE_DETECTED for obvious non-cyclic wave/task chains during VIDA 1.0 backlog slicing, preventing canonical ready queue ordering.
notes: mode=autonomous; reason=Initialized via framework-wave-start for dev-pack
close_reason: Autonomous next-task fallback added to preserve follow-through despite dependency false-cycle bug

title: Implementation Task - Source-tree ingest and migration bootstrap for DB-owned slices
description: Implement boot-time ingest from Git-resident source trees into DB-owned project, instruction, and framework slices with version metadata, hash-based change detection, and ordered migrations.
close_reason: Runtime-owned boot ingest for instruction and framework memory trees is implemented and proven

title: Architecture Task - Git source trees for project, instruction, and framework data slices
description: Define the Git-resident source tree layout for the three durable data slices, including nested directories, markdown/file support, metadata, and boot-time ingest into the DB-owned runtime.

title: Wave 4.4 - Selected-flow bridge replacement steps
description: Replace remaining bridge paths on the selected executable flows once route/receipt enforcement and resumable runtime behavior are stable.

title: Protocol Task - Library evaluation matrix and live-source selection rule
description: Define mandatory live-source library evaluation with alternatives matrix, freshness/liveness checks, and explicit winner selection before adopting third-party crates for core runtime behavior.

title: Agent-system Iteration 1 - Sources profiles taxonomy role contracts
description: Implement source registry/refresh, role profiles, taxonomy, and role contracts.

title: Protocol Task - User approval gate between autonomous tasks
description: Define a mode where before each next task the agent gathers specs and next-step context, performs meta-analysis, presents the proposed task for user approval outside TODO execution, then after approval updates specs and implements the task fully.

title: Research Epic - SurrealDB embedded storage fit and spec coverage
description: Track research and spec-coverage analysis for adopting SurrealDB embedded storage with kv-surrealkv in VIDA and beads-adjacent flows.

title: Agent-system Iteration 2 - Routing scoring adaptive scaling consensus packets handoffs verification
description: Implement routing, scoring, adaptive agent counts, consensus orchestration, task packets, handoffs, and verification burden.

title: Implementation Task - Effective instruction bundle service and ordered boot emission
description: Implement a system service that resolves the ordered effective instruction bundle for boot/runtime, emits mandatory instruction chain output in one pass, and records optional-condition activation separately.

title: Architecture Task - Define three-slice memory model for project memory, instruction memory, and sidecars
description: Define the canonical three-slice memory/storage model covering project memory, instruction-system memory, and instruction sidecars; include immutable framework-owned instruction bundles, migration/version checks, and sidecar override/replacement semantics.

description: Execute measured cutover from prior runtime paths to VIDA 1.0 primary runtime under explicit gates.

title: VIDA 1.0 direct binary release
description: Canonical root epic for delivering VIDA 1.0 through bounded implementation waves from Binary Foundation to Primary Switchover.

title: Wave 1 - Binary Foundation
description: Rust workspace, crate boundaries, minimal bootable vida binary, thin command shell, fast local proof loop, and temp-state harness seam.

title: Wave 1.1 - Minimal Rust workspace and bootable vida scaffold
description: Bring the staged Binary Foundation scaffold into the repository root as a Rust workspace with a bootable vida binary and fail-closed root command shell.
close_reason: Binary Foundation initial scaffold, proof loop, and fail-closed hardening are complete

title: Wave 1.2 - Cheap local proof loop for Binary Foundation
description: Establish and prove canonical local build/test/help commands for Binary Foundation via Makefile and staged proof path.
close_reason: Cheap local proof loop is captured and proven in tracked execution

title: Wave 1.3 - Fail-closed shell hardening
description: Cover unknown commands, bad boot arguments, and temp-state seam robustness without entering Wave 2 semantics.
close_reason: Fail-closed shell hardening is proven and reconciled in tracked execution

title: Wave 1.4 - Command shell diagnostics and fail-closed reporting polish
description: Polish root shell diagnostics, unknown command handling, and bounded operator-visible fail-closed messaging without entering Wave 2 semantics.
close_reason: Command-shell diagnostics and fail-closed reporting polish are proven in tracked execution

title: Wave 1.5 - Binary Foundation closure receipts and handoff readiness
description: Capture closure-ready proof, receipts, and handoff state for Binary Foundation so Wave 2 can start lawfully.
notes:
Wave 1 closure reconciliation: Wave 1.1-1.4 are already closed and collectively prove the Binary Foundation owns and delivers the Rust workspace, crate boundaries, bootable vida binary, thin command shell, temp-state harness seam, and cheap local proof loop. Closure-ready proof artifacts: Cargo workspace in root/Cargo.toml, vida crate in crates/vida, Makefile canonical proof loop targets, fail-closed CLI tests in crates/vida/tests/boot_smoke.rs, and successful cargo test proof after subsequent Wave 3 work. Handoff posture: Wave 2 may start lawfully because Wave 1 substrate is present, proven, and not blocked by unresolved Binary Foundation gaps; remaining work in Wave 1.5 is receipt/handoff bookkeeping only.

Closure-ready proof rerun for handoff: `make vida-build`, `make vida-test`, and `make vida-run-help` all pass against the current repo state. Handoff note: later Wave 3 operator-surface work extends the binary beyond the original Wave 1 non-goals, but it does not invalidate Binary Foundation closure; it strengthens the already-proven host substrate.
close_reason: Binary Foundation closure receipts and lawful Wave 2 handoff state are now captured and verified

title: VIDA silent diagnosis: verification capture after parent drift fix
Implementation Task - Architecture-aware optimized binary build targets
description: Add deterministic build/distribution support for per-architecture optimized VIDA binaries (for example x86_64 and aarch64), with explicit target labeling and no hidden CPU-extension assumptions in generic artifacts.

title: Wave 2 - State And Migration Backbone
description: Authoritative state spine, compatibility classification, migration preflight/runner, bridge import-export, and migration tests.

title: Wave 2.1 - Minimal authoritative state spine
description: Create the minimal binary-owned authoritative state entities and storage path needed to replace ad hoc temp-only state.
notes: Wave 2.1 closure summary: minimal authoritative state spine is now materially present as a DB-owned `state_spine_manifest` in embedded SurrealDB. The slice intentionally stays below compatibility classification and migration execution. Implemented scope: seed/read minimal authoritative spine summary, expose it in boot/status/doctor, preserve first initialization timestamp across reopen, and fail closed on missing or malformed manifest state. Proof: cargo test passes with unit coverage for manifest idempotency across reopen and missing-manifest failure, plus smoke coverage through boot/status/doctor surfaces. Review: external catch-review found no remaining remediation blockers after hardening.
close_reason: Minimal authoritative state spine is implemented, hardened, and verified

title: Wave 2.2 - Fail-closed boot compatibility classification
description: Classify startup compatibility and blocked boot outcomes before migration runner execution.
notes: Wave 2.2 progress summary: fail-closed boot compatibility classification is implemented and enforced. `boot` now stops on non-compatible classification, `status` and `doctor` surface the latest compatibility state, and `autonomous-next-task.py` no longer returns an empty selection merely because todo ui-json steps are absent. Hardening added a unit test for the incompatible path when instruction runtime state is missing. A direct external smoke test for command-level incompatible boot was attempted but ran into SurrealKV file-lock contention after intentional state corruption; the product logic is still covered by unit-level classification tests, while the lock behavior should be treated as a separate integration-testing constraint rather than folded into Wave 2.2 runtime law.
close_reason: Fail-closed boot compatibility classification is implemented, enforced, and verified

title: Wave 2.3 - Migration preflight and runner
description: Implement migration preflight, runner wiring, and blocked outcomes for incompatible state.

title: Wave 2.4 - Bridge import export path and migration tests
description: Add bridge import/export path and migration-focused tests once authoritative state and migration runner exist.

title: Wave 3 - Instruction And Operator Surface Integration
description: Instruction loading/composition, precedence activation runtime, and minimal operator-visible surfaces for boot/status/doctor/memory.

title: Wave 3.1 - Instruction loading and composition runtime
description: Load instruction artifacts and compose active instruction surfaces with preserved precedence.
notes: Wave 3.1 closure summary: instruction loading/composition runtime is materially present. Implemented scope includes instruction source ingest, dependency edge materialization from required_follow_on metadata, sidecar-backed artifact projection, deterministic mandatory-chain composition with cycle/missing-target fail-closed behavior, persisted effective bundle receipts, and DB-owned active instruction root selection via instruction_runtime_state. Hardening also preserves existing active root/runtime mode across bundle reseed so current runtime selection is not overwritten by framework defaults. Proof: cargo test passes with unit coverage for dependency cycles, missing targets, projection correctness, active-root loading, and root preservation across reseed; operator surfaces boot/memory/status/doctor resolve through the runtime-selected root rather than a literal artifact id. External catch-review reported no remaining remediation blockers after the reseed-preservation fix.
close_reason: Instruction loading and composition runtime are implemented, hardened, and verified

title: Wave 3.2 - Precedence activation runtime
description: Implement precedence-aware instruction activation runtime tied to canonical overlays and framework law.

title: Wave 3.3 - Minimal operator-visible boot status doctor memory surfaces
description: Expose minimal operator-visible boot/status/doctor/memory surfaces with fail-closed reporting, read-only inspection semantics where applicable, and operator-facing render modes (plain/color/color_emoji) with clean fallback behavior.
close_reason: Minimal operator-visible memory/status/doctor surfaces now work read-only, fail-closed, and support plain/color/color_emoji render modes

title: Wave 3.4 - Instruction and migration/operator failure reporting
description: Harden operator-visible reporting for instruction and migration state failures without entering Wave 4 task execution.

title: Wave 4 - Task Execution On Selected Flows
description: First real vida task execution flows, route/receipt enforcement on selected flows, resumable run-path wiring, and selected-flow bridge replacement steps.

title: Wave 4.1 - First selected vida task execution flow
description: Implement the first real selected vida task flow on top of the instruction and state backbones.

title: Wave 4.2 - Route and receipt enforcement on selected flows
description: Apply route and receipt enforcement to selected executable flows with fail-closed posture.

title: Wave 4.3 - Resumable run-path wiring
description: Wire resumable run-path behavior so selected flows can recover state and continue lawfully.

title: Wave 5 - Verification Visibility And Orchestration Hardening
description: Richer proofs, parity/conformance proving, trace/eval/operator visibility, and lawful adaptive orchestration payload integration.

title: Wave 6 - Primary Switchover
description: Measured cutover, rollback-safe switchover, and final promotion proofs for VIDA 1.0.

title: Agent-system payload integration across Waves A-F
description: Slice and integrate the autonomous role profiles and adaptive orchestration implementation plan as part of VIDA 1.0, not as a separate product.

title: Framework execution efficiency and autonomous follow-through hardening
description: Harden VIDA framework execution efficiency and autonomous follow-through, including approval-loop behavior, reporting continuity, mandatory catch-review coverage, protocol self-diagnosis, and other runtime protocol-drift corrections needed for long-running autonomous development.

title: Spec Task - Sidecar authoring and consistent projection rules for instruction updates
description: Define how an LLM or operator authors instruction-sidecar updates: delete, replace, insert, replace-with-many, anchor validation, and deterministic merge into one consistent effective output.
